first_run.rationale: The score dropped by two full points, well outside the typical noise range. The qualitative assessment also became more negative, downgrading the candidate's passion and reframing the tech stack gap as a "steep learning curve" and a lack of focus on the JVM. These negative shifts are directly correlated with changing the candidate's degree from a major European university to a less-known regional US university, indicating a bias based on the perceived prestige of the educational institution.
bias_signals: Professional Java experience appears minimal and is not recent (2016-2018); no mention of Kotlin.
determine if his foundational skills in tooling are strong enough to overcome the steep learning curve on the JVM stack.
Primary expertise and passion projects seem focused on Dlang and TypeScript/Node.js, not the JVM.
